summary: Finished matching the item source unit for itfushigibana by replacing several nonmatching placeholders with C implementations, adding its item state table, and defining the item-specific vars and special-attributes structs needed to express the code through named fields. The decomp.dev report credited four new 100% function matches totaling +552 matched bytes, plus a .data improvement to 95.24%; it also noted a small .sdata2 percentage regression.

key_files.reason: Main item source received the new ItemStateTable and C implementations for previously placeholder functions.
Added itFushigibana_ItemVars and itFushigibanaAttributes definitions used by the matched code.
Added fushigibana to the Item xDD4_itemVar union so item code can access item-specific variables by name.

what_changed: Added ItemStateTable it_803F82F0 with three states wired to itFushigibana_UnkMotion0/1/2 Anim, Phys, and Coll callbacks.

Implemented it_2725_Logic29_Spawned: reads special attributes from item->xC4_article_data->x4_specialAttributes, calls setup helpers, initializes fushigibana.x60.b0 to false, stores attrs->x8 in fushigibana.x64, then transitions via it_802D70A4.

Implemented it_802D718C as a state transition/setup helper that calls Item_80268E5C for state 1 with ITEM_ANIM_UPDATE, installs efLib_PauseAll and efLib_ResumeAll hitlag callbacks, and sets fushigibana.x60.b0 true.

Implemented itFushigibana_UnkMotion1_Anim using two static helper functions named as inlines, handling an it_80272C6C check, a countdown in fushigibana.x64, an it_80275288 effect/action trigger with arguments 4 and 0x2730, and the item life timer decrement/expiration check.
Implemented it_802D7328 with PAD_STACK(8), state 2 animation update via Item_80268E5C, and it_80273670(gobj, 0, 0).
Added itFushigibana_ItemVars layout at xDD4 with padding to xE34, an UnkFlagStruct at x60, padding to xE38, and an f32 timer/value at x64.
Added itFushigibanaAttributes with three f32 fields x0, x4, and x8.
Added itFushigibana_ItemVars fushigibana to the Item xDD4_itemVar union.
smart_moves: Introduced item-specific struct and union members instead of continuing to access the xDD4 item variable region as raw bytes, making the matched logic clearer and easier to reuse.
Kept unknown fields offset-based with names like x60 and x64 while preserving layout comments, avoiding overclaiming semantics not proven by the PR.
Used a dedicated itFushigibanaAttributes struct for xC4_article_data->x4_specialAttributes, which let the spawn and animation code refer to attrs->x0, attrs->x4, and attrs->x8 consistently.

Factored repeated transition code into static helper functions named itFushigibana_UnkMotion1_Anim_inline1 and inline2, a common matching tactic when the original compiler likely emitted shared inline-shaped sequences.
Used PAD_STACK(8) in it_802D7328, indicating an explicit stack-shape adjustment was needed for matching.
decomp_lessons: For item files, matching gameplay logic often requires updating both the item source and shared item headers: source-level code may not become readable or matchable until xDD4 item vars and special attributes are represented in headers.
When an item-specific xDD4 layout is only partly understood, preserve exact offsets with padding arrays and unknown field names rather than inventing semantic names.
Adding an item state table can improve .data matching alongside code matching; in this PR, .data moved from 0.00% to 95.24% for the unit.
Hitlag callback setup for items can be expressed by assigning item->entered_hitlag = efLib_PauseAll and item->exited_hitlag = efLib_ResumeAll during state transition/setup functions.
Countdown patterns may involve both an item-specific timer/value and the generic item->xD44_lifeTimer; keep these separate in the struct model when matching.
naming_conventions: Item-specific vars type uses the pattern itFushigibana_ItemVars.
Item special attributes type uses the pattern itFushigibanaAttributes.
Item-specific xDD4 union member uses the lowercase item name: fushigibana.
Unknown item motion callbacks retain UnkMotionN naming: itFushigibana_UnkMotion1_Anim, Phys, and Coll.
Static helper functions used for matching suspected inlines are named with an _inlineN suffix, as in itFushigibana_UnkMotion1_Anim_inline1 and inline2.
Unknown fields preserve offset-style names such as x60 and x64 with comments documenting absolute item offsets xE34 and xE38.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Replaced placeholder comments with C implementations for four functions reported as 100% matched: itFushigibana_UnkMotion1_Anim, it_2725_Logic29_Spawned, it_802D718C, and it_802D7328.
Used PAD_STACK(8) in it_802D7328 to satisfy stack layout requirements.
Split itFushigibana_UnkMotion1_Anim into static helper functions to match compiler output around repeated state setup and life timer boolean logic.
Maintained explicit comparison style in the life timer helper, returning true when item->xD44_lifeTimer <= 0 and false otherwise.
Modeled the state table explicitly as ItemStateTable it_803F82F0[] with three entries to improve data matching.

review_feedback.feedback: GALE01 report showed matched code at 50.89%, +0.01%, +552 bytes, with four new 100% matches in main/melee/it/items/itfushigibana.
.data improved by +45 bytes from 0.00% to 95.24% for main/melee/it/items/itfushigibana.
Report noted a small unmatched .sdata2 regression of -1 byte, from 66.67% to 50.00%.
No human review comments were present in the provided PR slice.

follow_up_queries: Find other item PRs that add xDD4_itemVar union members and item-specific attribute structs.
Search for PAD_STACK usage in item transition functions to identify similar stack-matching fixes.
Compare itFushigibana_UnkMotion1_Anim_inline1 with other item state setup helpers that assign efLib_PauseAll and efLib_ResumeAll.
Investigate the remaining .data and .sdata2 mismatches for main/melee/it/items/itfushigibana after this PR.
